verses.text: The psalmist's heart overflows with 'a good matter' concerning the King, 'fairer than the children of men,' His lips full of grace. He sings of one greater than Solomon. This is Christ, the altogether lovely King, gracious in word.

The psalmist's heart overflows with 'a good matter' concerning the King, 'fairer than the children of men,' His lips full of grace. He sings of one greater than Solomon. This is Christ, the altogether lovely King, gracious in word.

He bids the mighty King gird His sword and 'ride prosperously' for truth and righteousness, His arrows piercing His foes. The King goes forth to conquer. Christ advances His kingdom in truth and meekness, yet none can withstand Him.

He bids the mighty King gird His sword and 'ride prosperously' for truth and righteousness, His arrows piercing His foes. The King goes forth to conquer. Christ advances His kingdom in truth and meekness, yet none can withstand Him.

He bids the mighty King gird His sword and 'ride prosperously' for truth and righteousness, His arrows piercing His foes. The King goes forth to conquer. Christ advances His kingdom in truth and meekness, yet none can withstand Him.

'Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever,' for He loves righteousness and is anointed with gladness above His fellows. Here the King is called God. The New Testament applies this to the Son, whose eternal throne is sure.

'Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever,' for He loves righteousness and is anointed with gladness above His fellows. Here the King is called God. The New Testament applies this to the Son, whose eternal throne is sure.
His robes are fragrant with myrrh, and kings' daughters attend Him, the queen at His right hand in gold of Ophir. The royal wedding is splendid. The Church is the bride adorned for her glorious King.
His robes are fragrant with myrrh, and kings' daughters attend Him, the queen at His right hand in gold of Ophir. The royal wedding is splendid. The Church is the bride adorned for her glorious King.

The bride is told to forget her own people and cleave to the King, who greatly desires her beauty. New loyalty is required. To belong to Christ is to leave the old life and give the heart wholly to Him.

The bride is told to forget her own people and cleave to the King, who greatly desires her beauty. New loyalty is required. To belong to Christ is to leave the old life and give the heart wholly to Him.

The bride is told to forget her own people and cleave to the King, who greatly desires her beauty. New loyalty is required. To belong to Christ is to leave the old life and give the heart wholly to Him.

'The king's daughter is all glorious within,' clothed in wrought gold, brought to the King with gladness. Her true beauty is inward. The Church's loveliness is the righteousness her King has given her.

'The king's daughter is all glorious within,' clothed in wrought gold, brought to the King with gladness. Her true beauty is inward. The Church's loveliness is the righteousness her King has given her.

'The king's daughter is all glorious within,' clothed in wrought gold, brought to the King with gladness. Her true beauty is inward. The Church's loveliness is the righteousness her King has given her.

In place of her fathers shall be her children, princes in all the earth, and His name remembered for ever. The kingdom spreads through generations. The praise of Christ the King will never cease among the peoples.

In place of her fathers shall be her children, princes in all the earth, and His name remembered for ever. The kingdom spreads through generations. The praise of Christ the King will never cease among the peoples.
